claims.text: Five of six GSD II patients in this case series carry the c.1935C>A variant: P1 (compound het with c.1082C>T, both 'potentially less severe'), P2 and P3 (compound het with c.1216G>A, 'potentially less severe'), P5 (homozygous, 'potentially less severe'), and P6 (compound het with c.1822C>T 'very severe', c.1935C>A 'potentially less severe').

citations.quote: c.1082C>T, c.1935C>A (both potentially less severe) | c.1216G>A (unknow), c.1935C>A (potentially less severe) | c.1216G>A (unknow), c.1935C>A (potentially less severe) | c.1557G>A (unknown), c.1978C>T (potentially less severe) | homozygotic mutation of c.1935C>A (potentially less severe) | c.1822C>T (very severe), c.1935C>A (potentially less severe)

claims.text: Patient 2 (juvenile-onset, male) and Patient 3 (juvenile-onset, female) are siblings sharing the same genotype (compound heterozygous c.1216G>A / c.1935C>A), but the younger brother presented with more severe respiratory involvement and more difficult weaning than his older sister.

citations.quote: Patient 2, the younger brother of Patient 3, presented with severe respiratory involvement and weaning from mechanical ventilation proved more difficult than in his older sister. Patients 2 and 3 had the same genotype, but the younger brother who had higher GAA activity levels presented with severe respiratory involvement leading to more severe weaning difficulties.

claims.text: The paper asserts that five of six patients likely had CRIM-positive status because they carried the c.1935C>A mutation, which may explain their relatively better clinical outcomes.

citations.quote: We believe five of our six patients may have had a CRIM-positive status as they had the c.1935 C > A mutation [31]; this may be a reason for their better clinical outcomes.

claims.text: Patient 5, homozygous for c.1935C>A with zero GAA activity, was successfully weaned from mechanical ventilation twice, demonstrating no obligate correlation between GAA enzyme activity level and severity of respiratory symptoms.

citations.quote: patient 5, who had zero GAA activity, was successfully weaned from mechanical ventilation twice. This shows that there maybe no significant correlation between severe clinical manifestations such as respiratory symptoms and GAA enzyme activity.

claims.text: Yang et al. (reference 31) reported that 12 of 14 CRIM-positive infantile-onset Pompe disease patients carried the c.1935C>A variant, identifying it as the most commonly found mutation in that cohort.

citations.quote: Yang et al. [31] who reported on 14 infantile-onset patients who were CRIM-positive showed that 12/14 patients had the most commonly found mutation--c.1935 C > A.
